preview_text:
The model favors Croatia at 79% to win, a substantial edge over Ghana's 3% win probability. The forecast reflects a heavily asymmetric matchup, with the underdog facing minimal chances of outright victory on a neutral pitch.

The win-draw-loss distribution widens the gap further. Croatia's draw probability sits at 19%, meaning the model envisions roughly four outcomes in five going to a decisive result. Ghana's path to any positive result—whether a draw or victory—totals only 21%, suggesting the match is expected to lean decisively toward one team's control rather than remain competitive throughout.

Model and sportsbook readings diverge markedly. The market consensus prices Croatia at 58%, roughly 21 percentage points lower than the model's 79%. Conversely, the market assigns Ghana 17%, a sixfold increase over the model's 2.7%. Draw probabilities also widen by nearly 7 points. This gap suggests fundamentally different underlying assessments of the teams' relative strength. The dashboard presents both forecasts without adjudicating between them.
